meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve slow development speed
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to overcome slow development speed. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch faster.
content.answer_block: Slow development stems from over-engineering before validation. By implementing a tiered SaaS pricing model early, you force yourself to define core value propositions. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to iterate features against your pricing tiers, ensuring you build only what pays for itself.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a metronome counting down your wasted potential. You want to build a SaaS, but every feature feels like a mountain, and the finish line keeps receding into the fog of 'just one more feature.' This isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of constraints.

Real-world development isn't about writing the most code; it's about shipping the right code. By anchoring your development cycle to a SaaS pricing model, you transform abstract ideas into concrete, billable units. This shift moves you from a 'student coder' to a 'product engineer' who understands that speed is a feature, and simplicity is the ultimate leverage.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Before writing code, map out three pricing tiers. This prevents feature creep by forcing you to categorize every function as 'Core' or 'Premium'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my SaaS idea and propose 3 pricing tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise. For each, list exactly 3 core features that justify the price.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a markdown table of my pricing tiers and their associated feature sets.'

steps.title: Architect the Skeleton
steps.description: Build the minimum infrastructure required to support authentication and tier-based access control.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Next.js boilerplate with Supabase auth and a middleware structure that blocks access based on 'tier' metadata in the user profile.

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Connect your pricing model to real payment infrastructure. This validates your app's viability immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler in Next.js that updates a user's 'tier' in the database upon successful subscription payment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup Stripe checkout session and webhook listener for my defined tiers.'

steps.title: Feature Gating Logic
steps.description: Write a reusable hook that checks if a user has access to a specific UI component based on their current tier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook 'useAccess' that checks if the logged-in user's tier meets the minimum requirement for a given feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement useAccess hook throughout the dashboard to hide premium features for free users.'

steps.title: Rapid Iteration Loop
steps.description: Use Claude Code to add features one by one, ensuring each is tied to a specific tier and ready for production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current codebase and suggest the next highest-impact feature to add to the 'Pro' tier to increase conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze current project structure and suggest the next logical feature to implement.'

pro_tips: Always build the paywall first; if users won't pay for the core, don't waste time building the rest.
Use 'feature flags' instead of hard-coding tier logic to allow for instant pricing adjustments without redeploying.
Focus on 'Time to Value'—the faster a user hits the paywall, the faster you validate your business model.
Automate your database migrations using Claude Code to ensure your tier schema changes never break existing users.
Treat your pricing page as the most important UI component in your app; it is the primary driver of your architecture.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus on deploying your MVP to Vercel and collecting user feedback. Use the data from your first 10 users to iterate on your tier features.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting slow development hold your career back. By aligning your code with a revenue model, you'll ship faster and build a portfolio that stands out to any employer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
